beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: Almost none of the atoms in your hand were there five years ago. The body isn't an object — it's a pattern that matter pours through, like a flame. And trace any atom back far enough, and it was glowing inside a dying star billions of years before Earth formed.

card.copy: Your Body Is Stardust in Transit

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Almost none of the atoms in your hand were there five years ago. The body isn't an object — it's a pattern that matter pours through, like a flame. And trace an

beats.narration_text: The early universe produced only hydrogen, helium, and trace lithium. Carbon, oxygen, nitrogen, iron — the elements of biology — did not exist. They were forged in stellar interiors and scattered by explosions over billions of years.

production_viz.label: Periodic table lighting up — only H/He at Big Bang, then stellar synthesis routes adding C/O/N/Fe

beats.narration_text: Where exactly did the specific atoms making up your body come from — and how old are they?

card.copy: Carbon in your cells. Iron in your blood. Oxygen in your lungs. Where did each actually come from — and how old are they?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Where exactly did the specific atoms making up your body come from — and how old are they?

beats.narration_text: Carbon: the triple-alpha process in red giant cores. Three helium nuclei fuse into one carbon nucleus. When the red giant shed its outer layers, the carbon was released into space. Every carbon atom in your body passed through a dying star's core.

production_viz.label: Triple-alpha process: 3 helium nuclei fusing into carbon in red giant core — released into space

beats.narration_text: Iron: mostly from Type Ia supernovae. A white dwarf in a binary system accretes mass until it exceeds the Chandrasekhar limit and explodes, synthesizing iron-peak elements. The iron in your hemoglobin was forged in a supernova about 5 billion years ago.

production_viz.label: Type Ia supernova: white dwarf exceeding Chandrasekhar limit — iron-peak elements synthesized and scattered

beats.narration_text: Oxygen: fused in the cores of massive stars, released in core-collapse supernovae. Nitrogen: from the CNO cycle in intermediate-mass stars. Calcium in your bones and phosphorus in your DNA: from core-collapse supernovae.

production_viz.label: Element origin map: O from massive star cores, N from CNO cycle, Ca/P from core-collapse supernovae

beats.narration_text: The solar nebula — the cloud that collapsed to form the Sun and its planets — was enriched by generations of stellar death. Earth is built from the ashes of stars that lived and died before the Sun was born.

production_viz.label: Solar nebula formation from enriched stellar ash — generations of star death feeding into the cloud

beats.narration_text: Body replacement: water molecules are replaced every 16 days. About 98 percent of atoms are exchanged every year. You are not a fixed collection of atoms but a persistent pattern — a structure that maintains itself while matter flows through it.

production_viz.label: Body as flame diagram — matter flowing through persistent pattern; 16-day water and 1-year 98% atom turnover

beats.narration_text: The iron atom in your hemoglobin: synthesized in a supernova about 5 billion years ago. It drifted in interstellar space, condensed into the solar nebula, got incorporated into Earth, dissolved in ancient oceans, evolved through countless generations, and now carries oxygen in your blood. It will leave in 120 days.

production_viz.label: Single iron atom traced backward: hemoglobin → Earth → solar nebula → interstellar space → supernova 5 Gyr ago

beats.narration_text: 98 percent of body atoms replaced yearly. Water: 16 days. Carbon from triple-alpha in red giant cores. Iron from Type Ia supernovae, about 5 billion years old. You are a pattern matter pours through. Every atom has a cosmic history.

card.copy: Body: 98% atoms replaced yearly. Water: 16 days. Carbon: triple-alpha, red giant cores. Iron in hemoglobin: Type Ia supernova, ~5 Gyr old. You = pattern; atoms = travelers on cosmic timescales.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: 98 percent of body atoms replaced yearly. Water: 16 days. Carbon from triple-alpha in red giant cores. Iron from Type Ia supernovae, about 5 billion years old.

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
